Auth-hub sub-route slug read with a fixed sensible default (login / signup / verify-email). Only the hub base slug (buddynext_slug_auth) is admin-configurable; sub-routes use defaults plus a code-level update_option() override seam by design — no admin field intended. False positive.

Auth-hub sub-route slug read with a fixed sensible default (login / signup / verify-email). Only the hub base slug (buddynext_slug_auth) is admin-configurable; sub-routes use defaults plus a code-level update_option() override seam by design — no admin field intended. False positive.

Auth-hub sub-route slug read with a fixed sensible default (login / signup / verify-email). Only the hub base slug (buddynext_slug_auth) is admin-configurable; sub-routes use defaults plus a code-level update_option() override seam by design — no admin field intended. False positive.
One-way schema/version stamp written by the installer and consumed by the upgrade-check routine (and external migration tooling), not read back in the same scope. Intentional.
Distinct feature toggles (direct messaging vs spaces). The shared 'enable_' token is coincidental; they store different data. Not a dual key.
Distinct version stamps (rewrite/router version vs DB schema version) with separate lifecycles. Coincidental 'version' token. Not a dual key.

Recurring cron job scheduled via wp_schedule_event() in CronScheduler; the event is fired by WP-cron core (do_action inside wp-cron.php), not by plugin code, so the static scanner sees no firing. False positive.
